duration: 4 Years; 5 Years with integrated preparatory year
eligibility_criteria: Pakistan HSSC/Intermediate, normally Pre-Engineering, must be assessed as an international secondary qualification. Mathematics, physics and chemistry preparation is required. Because the HSSC represents 12 years of schooling, admission may include Polytechnique's integrated preparatory year or require additional recognized post-secondary study. Admission is competitive.
test_required: French test required for applicants educated outside a recognized francophone system. Accepted examples include TFI 785, DELF B2, DALF C1/C2, TCF 400 or TEF 541, subject to Polytechnique's current validity and component rules.
fee_structure: International: tuition is assessed per credit under Quebec international-student fee rules; mandatory institutional fees and health insurance are additional. The official calculator currently provides estimates for the academic year in force.

program: Génie chimique - Option Énergie et développement durable
eligibility_criteria: A recognized relevant bachelor's or engineering degree requiring at least four years of post-secondary study after secondary school. Program-specific prerequisites and a competitive academic record apply; Polytechnique makes the final equivalency decision.
test_required: French proficiency test required for applicants educated in a non-francophone system; professional master's and DESS programs use the same recognized French-test requirements as engineering bachelor's admission.
fee_structure: International: graduate tuition is assessed by cycle, registration status and credits under Quebec rules; mandatory institutional fees and health insurance are additional. Research students may qualify for funding or exemptions.

fee_structure: International: graduate tuition is assessed by cycle, registration status and credits under Quebec rules; mandatory institutional fees and health insurance are additional. Research students may qualify for funding or exemptions.

eligibility_criteria: A recognized relevant bachelor's or engineering degree requiring at least four years of post-secondary study after secondary school. Program-specific prerequisites and a competitive academic record apply; Polytechnique makes the final equivalency decision.
test_required: French proficiency test required for applicants educated in a non-francophone system; professional master's and DESS programs use the same recognized French-test requirements as engineering bachelor's admission.
fee_structure: International: graduate tuition is assessed by cycle, registration status and credits under Quebec rules; mandatory institutional fees and health insurance are additional. Research students may qualify for funding or exemptions.

department: Département des arts, lettres et du langage
program: Baccalauréat avec majeure en études littéraires françaises
eligibility_criteria: Pakistan Intermediate/HSSC (12 years) plus at least one year of recognized university study; UQAC normally requires the equivalent of 13 years of prior schooling. Program-specific academic prerequisites and competitive selection rules apply.
test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 25,000/year (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program/credit-specific. Mandatory institutional fees (~CAD 650/term) and international health insurance (~CAD 300/term) are additional.

fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 25,000/year (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program/credit-specific. Mandatory institutional fees (~CAD 650/term) and international health insurance (~CAD 300/term) are additional.

test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life. Some graduate programs also require functional academic English.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 23,600/year before exemption; approx. CAD 5,600/year after the UQAC doctoral tuition-exemption scholarship if eligible (latest official planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates). Mandatory fees and insurance are additional.

test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life. Some graduate programs also require functional academic English.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 18,000-27,000/year depending on research or professional profile (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program-specific. Mandatory fees and insurance are additional.

department: Département des sciences humaines et sociales
program: Programme court individualisé de deuxième cycle pour étudiants en séjour d'études
eligibility_criteria: Study-abroad/exchange pathway only; not a standalone UQAC degree admission. The student must be enrolled at a home university, be nominated/approved under an exchange or mobility arrangement, and obtain UQAC program approval.
test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life. Some graduate programs also require functional academic English.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 18,000-27,000/year depending on research or professional profile (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program-specific. Mandatory fees and insurance are additional.

program: Microprogramme de deuxième cycle en développement de jeux vidéo

test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life. Some graduate programs also require functional academic English.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 18,000-27,000/year depending on research or professional profile (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program-specific. Mandatory fees and insurance are additional.

program: Microprogramme de deuxième cycle en gestion publique en contexte autochtone

fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 25,000/year (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program/credit-specific. Mandatory institutional fees (~CAD 650/term) and international health insurance (~CAD 300/term) are additional.

eligibility_criteria: Study-abroad/exchange pathway only; not a standalone UQAC degree admission. The student must be enrolled at a home university, be nominated/approved under an exchange or mobility arrangement, and obtain UQAC program approval.
test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 25,000/year (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program/credit-specific. Mandatory institutional fees (~CAD 650/term) and international health insurance (~CAD 300/term) are additional.

program: Programme court sur les modèles/approches d'interv. contexte éducatif auprès de la personne autiste ou ayant une déficience intel.
eligibility_criteria: Pakistan Intermediate/HSSC (12 years) plus at least one year of recognized university study; UQAC normally requires the equivalent of 13 years of prior schooling. Program-specific academic prerequisites and competitive selection rules apply. Quebec teacher-certification and practicum conditions apply to teacher-education programs.
test_required: French B2 for identified programs: TCF or TEF 400 in oral and written comprehension, Bright 3.5 in both, or DELF B2. Test generally valid 24 months; DELF is valid for life. Teacher-education French assessments/TECFÉE requirements may also apply after admission.
fee_structure: International: approx. CAD 25,000/year (latest official UQAC planning estimate based on Fall 2025 rates); exact 2026-27 tuition is program/credit-specific. Mandatory institutional fees (~CAD 650/term) and international health insurance (~CAD 300/term) are additional.

eligibility_criteria: HSSC (FSc/ICS/Pre-Engineering) or A-Levels with minimum 70% marks in Mathematics and Physics/Science. Competitive applicants typically score 80%+ in HSSC. International students must complete a 1-year Preparatory Year (Année préparatoire) unless they hold a DEC technique (Quebec technical college diploma). Strong Mathematics and Physics background essential. Admission is open (no quota) — ÉTS does not apply enrollment limits.
test_required: French language proficiency required: DELF B2 or TEF/TCF equivalent (ÉTS is a French-medium institution). English: IELTS 6.0 or TOEFL iBT 80 (for English documentation only). Note: All instruction is in French. French proficiency is mandatory for all undergraduate programs.
fee_structure: Quebec residents: CAD ~2,500/year | Other Canadian: CAD ~9,000/year | International: CAD ~26,500/year (excluding Preparatory Year fees ~CAD 10,000)

eligibility_criteria: HSSC (FSc/ICS/Pre-Engineering) or A-Levels with minimum 70% marks in Mathematics and Physics/Science. Competitive applicants typically score 80%+ in HSSC. International students must complete a 1-year Preparatory Year (Année préparatoire) unless they hold a DEC technique (Quebec technical college diploma). Strong Mathematics and Physics background essential. Admission is open (no quota) — ÉTS does not apply enrollment limits.
test_required: French language proficiency required: DELF B2 or TEF/TCF equivalent (ÉTS is a French-medium institution). English: IELTS 6.0 or TOEFL iBT 80 (for English documentation only). Note: All instruction is in French. French proficiency is mandatory for all undergraduate programs.
fee_structure: Quebec residents: CAD ~2,500/year | Other Canadian: CAD ~9,000/year | International: CAD ~26,500/year (excluding Preparatory Year fees ~CAD 10,000)

eligibility_criteria: Bachelor's degree (BEng or equivalent, 16 years of education) in a relevant engineering or science field from an HEC-recognised institution with minimum GPA 3.0/4.3 or 65% marks. MEng (project profile) is practice-oriented; MASc (thesis profile) is research-oriented and requires a faculty supervisor. Transcripts, statement of purpose, CV, and two references required. Candidates who are inadequately prepared may be required to take upgrading courses.

fee_structure: Quebec residents: CAD ~5,500/year | Other Canadian: CAD ~10,000/year | International: CAD ~18,000/year. MASc (research) students may be eligible for funding from supervisor research grants.
